objectives: Full security audit of MX20022 payments runtime workspace post-remediation commit 70f8383
Cover OWASP Top 10, crypto, auth, input validation, CI supply chain, TLS, rate limiting
constraints: Rust workspace, no unsafe blocks found in crate source
important_facts: CRITICAL: CryptoService HKDF salt derived from master_key itself (SHA-256 of master_key used as salt) - circular derivation reduces KDF security
CRITICAL: Bearer token comparisons (legacy auth + static bearer channel auth) use non-constant-time == operator - timing side-channel risk
HIGH: CORS allows Any origin on admin host AND HTTP inbound channel - credential-bearing endpoints exposed to cross-origin attacks
HIGH: JWT HS256 secret stored as plaintext String in config structs in memory - no zeroization on drop
HIGH: TCP channel auth_token compared with non-constant-time != operator
HIGH: No TLS enforcement on any channel (HTTP, gRPC, TCP, Kafka, AMQP, NATS) - all listen plaintext by default
HIGH: Admin auth defaults to Disabled mode - no auth unless explicitly configured
HIGH: Channel inbound auth defaults to Disabled mode - no auth unless explicitly configured
MEDIUM: Example config docs/examples/basic.toml ships jwt_hs256_secret = 'replace-me' - trivially guessable placeholder
MEDIUM: Config file stores secrets (jwt_hs256_secret, legacy_bearer_token, auth_bearer_token, auth_jwt_hs256_secret) in plaintext TOML
MEDIUM: Kafka consumer uses enable.auto.commit=true - message loss risk on crash
MEDIUM: Store URL (may contain DB credentials) passed as plaintext string through config
MEDIUM: RocksDB store uses from_utf8_lossy for key comparison - silent data corruption possible
MEDIUM: No rate limiting on admin endpoints (middleware RateLimit stage is a no-op)
LOW: deny.toml ignores RUSTSEC-2024-0384 and RUSTSEC-2025-0134 advisories
LOW: CI workflow uses dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable without SHA pinning
LOW: CI uses Swatinem/rust-cache@v2 without SHA pinning
LOW: File channel does not validate path traversal in pattern matching
INFO: No unsafe blocks in any crate source code
INFO: All SQL queries use parameterized bindings (no SQL injection risk)
INFO: Security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, nosniff, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy) added to admin and HTTP channel
INFO: Body size limits applied (10MB max on admin and HTTP channel)
INFO: Secrets redacted from Debug output on AuthConfig and InboundAuthConfig
INFO: CryptoService key_bytes zeroed on Drop
risks_issues: Timing side-channel on token comparison in production payment auth paths
Default-disabled auth means misconfiguration leads to unauthenticated access
No TLS enforcement means network eavesdropping of payment messages is possible
CORS Any origin on credential endpoints enables CSRF-like attacks

iteration.summary: Completed comprehensive re-audit of all crates post-remediation commit 70f8383. Previous CRITICAL finding (hardcoded static HKDF salt) was partially fixed - salt is now SHA-256 of master_key, but this is a circular derivation that does not add independent entropy. Previous HIGH finding (legacy bearer accepts any non-empty token) was fully fixed - now requires explicit token configuration. Security headers and body limits were added. However, new and remaining findings include: timing side-channel on all token comparisons, CORS wildcard on credential endpoints, default-disabled auth on all paths, no TLS enforcement on channels, plaintext secrets in config, and CI action pinning gaps. Total: 2 CRITICAL, 6 HIGH, 6 MEDIUM, 4 LOW, 5 INFORMATIONAL findings.
next_actions: Use constant_time_eq (from subtle crate) for all token/secret comparisons
Restrict CORS to explicit allowed origins or remove Any
Add independent salt to HKDF (e.g., random per-deployment salt stored alongside config)
Implement zeroize for JWT secrets in config structs
Add startup warning/error when auth is disabled in non-dev environments
Enforce TLS or document network isolation requirements per channel
Support env var / secret manager references in config instead of plaintext secrets
Pin CI actions to SHA digests

mentions.relevance_note: Architecture decisions on auth defaults and channel security posture are security-relevant
Missing security test coverage for timing attacks and CORS behavior
